issues.issue_question: Will softer growth change near-term Fed expectations?
issues.thesis_hint: Growth is cooling while policy stays cautious.

claims.text: Softer growth is raising later-cut expectations.
claims.why_it_matters: Rate-sensitive assets remain exposed to data surprises.

claims.text: Steady policy language can still slow repricing.
claims.why_it_matters: Policy communication still anchors the near-term debate.

claims.text: Softer spending could cool the growth scare faster than markets expect.
claims.why_it_matters: Consumer demand can challenge the dominant market read.

claims.text: Watch the next payroll release for confirmation that labor demand is easing.
claims.why_it_matters: The next labor print can confirm or break the easing thesis.

brief_thesis_contains: Officials said inflation progress remains uneven
Payroll growth moderated and wage gains cooled.
html_contains: Will softer growth change near-term Fed expectations?
Softer growth is raising later-cut expectations.
